findings.title: Notification Priority Levels Implementation
findings.description: Implementing a three-tier priority system for notifications requires changes to the notification rendering logic and user interface components. This will also necessitate updates to the backend to support priority levels.
assumptions: The existing notification system can be modified to support priority levels.

evidence.excerpt: Propose a three-tier priority system that visually differentiates urgency.

recommendations: Design and implement the priority levels in the notification system.

findings.title: Push Notification Delivery Issues on Android 14
findings.description: Push notifications are failing to deliver on Android 14 devices, affecting 8% of users. This is a critical issue that needs to be resolved to ensure reliable notification delivery.
assumptions: The issue is solely related to Android 14 and can be fixed with a software update.

evidence.excerpt: Several users on Android 14 report that push notifications silently fail.

recommendations: Investigate and resolve the push notification delivery issue for Android 14.

findings.title: Implement ML-based Notification Batching
findings.description: To reduce notification fatigue, implementing ML-based batching of low-priority notifications is recommended. This will require developing an ML model and integrating it with the notification delivery system.
assumptions: The ML model can be trained effectively with existing user engagement data.

evidence.excerpt: Batch low-priority notifications into a digest delivered at optimal times.

recommendations: Develop the ML model and integrate it with the notification system.

findings.title: User Preference Page for Notification Categories
findings.description: Creating a user preference page requires a new API endpoint and a React component. This will also need to respect existing global mute settings.
assumptions: The existing backend can support the new preferences API.

evidence.excerpt: Users should be able to opt in/out of specific notification categories.

recommendations: Design and implement the user preference page and API.

findings.title: Notification Delivery Latency
findings.description: The current p95 notification delivery latency exceeds the SLO of 200ms. This is a significant performance issue that needs to be addressed to improve user experience.
assumptions: The proposed fix of moving to async writes and batch inserts will effectively reduce latency.

evidence.excerpt: Performance monitoring shows p95 notification delivery latency at 340ms.

recommendations: Implement async writes and batch inserts to reduce latency.

summary: The analysis reveals critical dependencies and risks associated with the implementation of the SmartNotify system. Key areas of focus include resolving push notification delivery issues on Android 14, addressing notification delivery latency, and implementing user preferences and ML-based batching. A phased delivery plan is proposed to prioritize critical fixes and enhancements.
